apps.name: Cloudflare Tunnel
apps.tagline: Expose local services to the internet securely via Cloudflare
apps.description: Cloudflare Tunnel creates a secure outbound connection from your server to Cloudflare's network — no inbound firewall rules or public IPs needed. Get your tunnel token from the Cloudflare Zero Trust dashboard.
apps.compose:
services:
  cloudflared:
    image: cloudflare/cloudflared:latest
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: tunnel --no-autoupdate run --token ${TUNNEL_TOKEN}

env.label: Tunnel Token
env.description: Found in Cloudflare Zero Trust dashboard → Networks → Tunnels → your tunnel → Configure → Install connector.

apps.name: WireGuard Easy
apps.tagline: WireGuard VPN with a simple web UI
apps.description:
WG-Easy is the easiest way to run WireGuard VPN + a web UI for managing peers. Requires your server's public IP or hostname and a password hash.

Generate password hash on your server:
  docker run --rm ghcr.io/wg-easy/wg-easy:15 wgpw 'your-password'
apps.compose:
services:
  wg-easy:
    image: ghcr.io/wg-easy/wg-easy:15
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      - WG_HOST=${WG_HOST}
      - WG_PORT=${WG_PORT}
      - WG_DEFAULT_DNS=${WG_DEFAULT_DNS}
    ports:
      - "${WG_PORT}:${WG_PORT}/udp"
      - "${WEBUI_PORT}:51821/tcp"
    volumes:
      - wg_data:/etc/wireguard
    cap_add:
      - NET_ADMIN
      - SYS_MODULE
    sysctls:
      - net.ipv4.conf.all.src_valid_mark=1
      - net.ipv4.ip_forward=1

volumes:
  wg_data:

env.label: Public Host
env.description: Public IP address or hostname of your server. Clients use this to connect.

env.label: WireGuard Port (UDP)
env.description: UDP port WireGuard listens on. Must be open in your firewall.

env.label: Web UI Port
env.description: Host port to expose the WG-Easy web UI on.

env.label: Default DNS
env.description: DNS server pushed to VPN clients.

apps.name: Beszel
apps.tagline: Lightweight server and Docker monitoring hub
apps.description: Beszel is a lightweight server monitoring platform with a web UI for tracking CPU, memory, disk, and Docker container stats across multiple systems.
apps.compose:
services:
  beszel:
    image: henrygd/beszel:latest
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      - APP_URL=${APP_URL}
    ports:
      - "${PORT}:8090"
    volumes:
      - beszel_data:/beszel_data

volumes:
  beszel_data:

env.label: App URL
env.description: The URL you will use to access Beszel (e.g. http://your-server-ip:8090 or your domain).

env.label: Port
env.description: Host port to expose the Beszel web UI on.

apps.name: MySpeed
apps.description: MySpeed saves your internet speed test results over time so you can track performance trends, detect outages, and monitor your ISP's reliability — all from a clean self-hosted dashboard.
apps.compose:
services:
  myspeed:
    image: germannewsmaker/myspeed:latest
    container_name: myspeed
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "${PORT}:5216"
    volumes:
      - myspeed_data:/myspeed/data

volumes:
  myspeed_data:

env.label: Port
env.description: Host port to expose the MySpeed web UI on.

apps.name: Scrutiny
apps.tagline: Hard drive S.M.A.R.T. health monitoring with historical trends
apps.description:
Scrutiny monitors the S.M.A.R.T. health of all your hard drives and SSDs, surfacing failure predictions and historical trends in a clean web dashboard. The omnibus image bundles the web UI and InfluxDB time-series store in a single container.

After installing, add your disk devices to the compose file — by default /dev/sda is included. Add more entries (e.g. /dev/sdb, /dev/nvme0n1) as needed.
apps.compose:
services:
  scrutiny:
    image: ghcr.io/analogj/scrutiny:master-omnibus
    restart: unless-stopped
    cap_add:
      - SYS_RAWIO
    ports:
      - "${PORT}:8080"
      - "${INFLUXDB_PORT}:8086"
    volumes:
      - /run/udev:/run/udev:ro
      - scrutiny_config:/opt/scrutiny/config
      - scrutiny_influxdb:/opt/scrutiny/influxdb
    devices:
      - /dev/sda
      # Add more drives: /dev/sdb, /dev/nvme0n1, etc.

volumes:
  scrutiny_config:
  scrutiny_influxdb:

env.label: Web UI Port
env.description: Host port to expose the Scrutiny web UI on.

env.label: InfluxDB Port
env.description: Host port for the bundled InfluxDB instance. Only needed if you want direct InfluxDB access; otherwise leave at the default.

apps.name: Umami
apps.description:
Umami is a simple, fast, privacy-focused alternative to Google Analytics. No cookies, no personal data, GDPR compliant by design. Tracks page views, visitors, referrers, devices, and custom events.

After installing, open the web UI and log in with the default credentials (admin / umami) — change the password immediately. Then create a website to get your Website ID and tracking script.
apps.compose:
services:
  umami:
    image: ghcr.io/umami-software/umami:postgresql-latest
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "${PORT}:3000"
    environment:
      - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://umami:${DB_PASSWORD}@db:5432/umami
      - DATABASE_TYPE=postgresql
      - APP_SECRET=${APP_SECRET}
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy

  db:
    image: postgres:15-alpine
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_DB=umami
      - POSTGRES_USER=umami
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${DB_PASSWORD}
    volumes:
      - umami_db:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U umami"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  umami_db:

env.label: Port
env.description: Host port to expose the Umami web UI on.

env.label: Database Password
env.description: Password for the internal PostgreSQL database. Use a strong random string.

env.label: App Secret
env.description: Random secret used to sign session tokens. Use a long random string.

apps.name: QwenPaw
apps.tagline: Self-hosted personal AI assistant with multi-agent, skills, and memory
apps.description:
QwenPaw is a self-hosted personal AI assistant built on Qwen models. Run it on your own server for full data control — memory, skills, and configuration never leave your machine.

Features multi-agent collaboration, extensible skills (scheduling, document processing, news digests), integrations with Telegram, Discord, WeChat, DingTalk, and Feishu, and persistent memory that evolves with use.

After installing, open the web console and configure your model provider API key in Settings → Model Provider. Enable authentication (recommended) via the environment variables below.
apps.compose:
services:
  qwenpaw:
    image: agentscope/qwenpaw:latest
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "${PORT}:8088"
    environment:
      - QWENPAW_AUTH_ENABLED=${AUTH_ENABLED}
      - QWENPAW_AUTH_USERNAME=${AUTH_USERNAME}
      - QWENPAW_AUTH_PASSWORD=${AUTH_PASSWORD}
      - DASHSCOPE_API_KEY=${DASHSCOPE_API_KEY}
    volumes:
      - qwenpaw_data:/app/working
      - qwenpaw_secrets:/app/working.secret
      - qwenpaw_backups:/app/working.backups

volumes:
  qwenpaw_data:
  qwenpaw_secrets:
  qwenpaw_backups:

env.label: Port
env.description: Host port to expose the QwenPaw web console on.

env.label: DashScope API Key
env.description: Alibaba Cloud DashScope API key for Qwen model access. Get one at dashscope.aliyun.com. You can also configure other model providers from the web console after install.

env.label: Enable Authentication
env.description: Set to 'true' to require login to access the web console. Recommended when exposing to a network.

env.label: Admin Username
env.description: Username for the web console login. Only used when authentication is enabled.

env.label: Admin Password
env.description: Password for the web console login. Use a strong password. Only used when authentication is enabled.

apps.name: Uptime Kuma
apps.tagline: Self-hosted uptime monitoring tool with a fancy UI
apps.description: Uptime Kuma is an easy-to-use self-hosted monitoring tool for tracking uptime of websites, TCP ports, DNS, and more with notifications support.
apps.compose:
services:
  uptime-kuma:
    image: louislam/uptime-kuma:2
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "${PORT}:3001"
    volumes:
      - uptime_kuma_data:/app/data

volumes:
  uptime_kuma_data:

env.label: Port
env.description: Host port to expose the Uptime Kuma web UI on.
